title: Non-Title-18-C citations the probate forms touch
note: Curated cross-reference table for statutes outside Title 18-C that the per-form statute-consideration layer may cite. Citations here are also accepted by scripts/verify_statutes.py. Verified against legislature.maine.gov / Maine Revenue Services as of 2026-05.

36 M.R.S. §4102.title: Maine estate tax (after 2012): definitions; Maine exclusion amount
36 M.R.S. §4102.note: Chapter 577 governs estates of decedents dying on or after 2013-01-01. The Maine exclusion amount determines whether any estate tax is owed.

36 M.R.S. §4103.title: Maine estate tax: tax on estate of resident
36 M.R.S. §4103.note: Imposes the Maine estate tax on a resident decedent's Maine taxable estate above the exclusion amount.

36 M.R.S. §4104.title: Maine estate tax: tax on estate of nonresident
36 M.R.S. §4104.note: Applies to Maine-situs real and tangible personal property of a nonresident decedent.

36 M.R.S. §4107.title: Maine estate tax: tax due date; filing of return and payment
36 M.R.S. §4107.note: Return and payment are due 9 months after the date of death. This is the operative estate-tax deadline an estate-administration filer must track.

36 M.R.S. §4063.title: Former Maine estate tax (pre-2013)
36 M.R.S. §4063.note: SUPERSEDED for deaths on or after 2013-01-01 by Chapter 577 (36 M.R.S. §§4101-4118). Some legacy form annotations cite §4063; for any current estate use §4102/§4103/§4107 instead.

18 U.S.C. §2701 et seq..title: Federal Stored Communications Act (SCA)
18 U.S.C. §2701 et seq..note: Federal privacy law that restricts custodians (Google, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, etc.) from disclosing the contents of electronic communications without lawful consent or process. It is the reason a personal representative often cannot get message/email CONTENT on letters alone and a provider may require a U.S. court order. Maine RUFADAA (18-C §10-107) is the state-law counterpart that supplies the consent/authority pathway.

M.R. Prob. P..title: Maine Rules of Probate Procedure
M.R. Prob. P..note: Court rules governing service, notice, motions, and practice in the Probate Courts. Procedural companion to Title 18-C; consult the current rules for service/notice mechanics referenced by many forms.

19-A M.R.S..title: Maine domestic relations law (incl. UCCJEA)
19-A M.R.S..note: Where a minor is the subject of a guardianship or name-change petition and a parental rights/custody matter is or could be pending, jurisdiction and competing custody determinations may be governed by Title 19-A (including the Uniform Child Custody Jurisdiction and Enforcement Act). Relevant to the jurisdictional affidavits (AF-101) and minor name change (NC-001).

22 M.R.S..title: Maine health and welfare law (DHHS; child welfare; vital records)
22 M.R.S..note: Adoption practice intersects Title 22 (Department of Health and Human Services placement, the adoption registry, and vital records / birth certificate amendment). Relevant to the AD-* adoption forms.
